mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in consumer credit outstanding and a decline in housing starts indicates a synchronized withdrawal of household balance sheet leverage, which suppresses aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its historical trend while inflation remains elevated, supply-side bottlenecks persist, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn positive when INDPRO and CPIAUCSL both remain above trend, or the mechanism loses predictive power once supply chain pressures normalize.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, directly boosting earnings multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if GDPC1 growth decelerations consistently coincide with positive SPX forward returns, or if the rolling Spearman correlation between GDP growth changes and 5-day returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, borrowing costs exceed the historical threshold for corporate debt servicing capacity, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite rates remaining above 5.0%, or forward returns show no statistical difference between periods above and below the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth deceleration and a rising unemployment rate confirm a structural labor market cooling, which reduces consumer spending power and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE rise fail to predict negative returns, or single-series signals dominate the interaction effect in a stepwise regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery where real GDP growth accelerates above 2.5% while unemployment falls below 4.0%, demand-pull inflation expectations rise, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with tight labor markets
failure_condition: The mechanism fails when strong GDP growth coincides with falling equity returns due to rate hike fears, or when the combined signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.
